channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump administration plans to end free tax filing program
item.description: But, in mid-March, the IRS staff assigned to the program were told to stop working on it for the 2026 tax filing season, the two sources told the AP.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: Police records destroy media narrative about ‘wrongly deported’ Maryland migrant
item.description: The Department of Homeland Security released court records it says proves Salvadoran migrant Kilmar Abrego Garcia is a member of the infamous MS-13 gang.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump joins tariff talks with Japan as US seeks deals amid trade wars
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump on Wednesday inserted himself directly into trade talks with Japanese officials, a sign of the high stakes for the United States after its tariffs rattled the economy and caused the administration to assure the public that it would quickly reach deals.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, MARI YAMAGUCHI and JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump admin asks IRS to revoke Harvard's tax-exempt status
item.description: The Trump administration has asked the Internal Revenue Service to strip Harvard University of its tax-exempt status amid accusations of antisemitism on campus.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What happens next after judge warns of possible contempt prosecution over deportation flights order
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge's ruling that the Trump administration appears to have willfully violated his order to turn around planes of migrants headed for El Salvador increases the prospect of officials being held in criminal contempt of court and potentially facing possible prosecution. U.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, MARK SHERMAN and MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rachel Morin's mom shares horrific details of daughter's murder, slams her senator for trip to El Salvador
item.description: Patty Morin’s appearance comes as Democratic Sen. Chris Van Hollen visited El Salvador on Wednesday to check on a Maryland resident deported to El Salvador.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US imposes sanctions on a Chinese refinery accused of buying Iranian oil
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Treasury Department imposed sanctions Wednesday on a Chinese refinery accused of purchasing more than $1 billion worth of Iranian oil, saying the proceeds help finance both Tehran's government and Iran's support for militant groups.
item.dc:creator: DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The State Department closes the office that flags disinformation from Russia, China and Iran
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The State Department has shut down its office that sought to deal with misinformation and disinformation that Russia, China and Iran have been accused of spreading.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge finds probable cause to hold Trump administration in contempt for violating deportation order
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge on Wednesday said he has found probable cause to hold the Trump administration in criminal contempt of court for violating his orders last month to turn around planes carrying deportees to an El Salvador prison. The ruling from U.S District Judge James E.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Powerful House leadership chair, Stefanik poised to take on Hochul for NY governor
item.description: New York GOP Rep. Elise Stefanik is considering a bid for governor in 2026, multiple sources close to her confirmed to Fox News Digital following a Wall Street Journal report.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Vance to meet Modi, Meloni during trip to India and Italy with wife Usha
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Vice President JD Vance and his family will travel to Italy and India this week and next to meet with leaders and visit cultural sites.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Kentucky senate race for McConnell’s seat heats up
item.description: Kentucky Rep. Andy Barr posted millions in first quarter fundraising Tuesday as the senate race for the soon vacant seat of Sen. Mitch McConnell continues.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Grassley, Johnson press DOJ IG to respond on whether ‘untasked’ undercover agents were present on Jan 6
item.description: EXCLUSIVE: Sens. Ron Johnson and Chuck Grassley are demanding information from the Justice Department’s inspector general on whether any unassigned, or "untasked," confidential human sources from DOJ agencies beyond the FBI were in or around the Capitol on Jan. 6, 2021.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Newsom says California will sue over Trump tariffs
item.description: “President Trump’s unlawful tariffs are wreaking chaos on California families, businesses, and our economy — driving up prices and threatening jobs," Newsom said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Maryland senator to visit El Salvador to check on mistakenly deported man
item.description: Maryland Sen. Chris Van Hollen (D) said he is traveling to El Salvador on Wednesday to check on the status of a deported legal resident.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Voucher vote updates: Bid to send voucher plan for statewide vote is defeated
item.description: After the Texas House approved more money for schools in a bipartisan vote, it began debate on school vouchers.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Jaden Edison And Jasper Scherer, KPRC 2

item.title: Marshall man claims ICE detained him for protests over police killings
item.description: A Marshall man was detained by federal immigration authorities who claim he overstayed his student visa after it was revoked. However, he tells MPR News from the Kandiyohi County Jail that his arrest may have been politically motivated.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Yang, MPR News

item.title: Trump presents Commander-in-Chief's trophy to US Navy
item.description: The award is presented annually to the team that wins the college football series among the Army, Navy and Air Force academies.
item.dc:creator: The Hill Staff, NewsNation

item.title: Trump not expected to attend White House Correspondents' dinner
item.description: Trump did not attend any of the annual dinners during his first term, bucking a tradition that went back several administrations.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, NewsNation

item.title: Big law spends top dollar as Trump takes aim at firms
item.description: A series of new agreements brokered between President Trump and major law firms suggests a new price tag for those looking to dodge executive orders targeting the legal profession.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Minnesota Senate Minority Leader Johnson is optimistic lawmakers will make session deadline
item.description: State lawmakers have a big hill to climb with just over a month before the Legislative session is over. Despite economic uncertainty, Senate Minority Leader Mark Johnson, R-East Grand Forks, said he’s optimistic they’ll get a budget passed before the deadline.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer, Lukas Levin, and Gracie Stockton, MPR News

item.title: Tariffs could impact Tampa Bay shoppers, businesses by summer, economist says
item.description: TAMPA, Fla. (WFLA) — Cheri Hudson owns clothing store Imagine That!!! in South Tampa. It has been her business for 25 years. She is a supporter of President Trump’s trade policies. “I trust Donald Trump. It's only going to be a matter of time. The tariffs are fair.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Bondi appoints new interim U.S. Attorney for San Diego, Imperial counties
item.description: The Southern District of California will have a new U.S attorney after Attorney General Pam Bondi appointed a longtime San Diego prosecutor to take over the role as the region's top federal law enforcement official following Tara McGrath's termination.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, Fox 5

item.title: What to know about Dan Caine, the nation's newly-confirmed top military adviser
item.description: The Senate voted around 2 a.m. Friday to confirm retired Air Force Lt. Gen. Dan Caine as chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff. Trump plucked him from retirement to be his top military adviser.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman, Juliana Kim, and Tom Bowman, MPR News

item.title: Las Vegas movie studio plan changing with new players, tech/education emphasis
item.description: A global player, MBS Group, is stepping into the plan for a $900 million movie studio at the UNLV Harry Reid Research and Technology Park, near the 215 Beltway and Durango Drive in the southwest Las Vegas valley. Birtcher Development LLC is the project's developer.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Nevada Legislature moving bills as Friday deadline nears; ghost kitchens, open enrollment among winners
item.description: Up against a Friday deadline for bills to be passed out of committee, Nevada lawmakers are working this week to move mountains of legislation in Carson City.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump must face Central Park 5 defamation suit, judge rules
item.description: A federal judge declined to toss a defamation lawsuit against President Trump over his remarks last year about five Black and Hispanic men wrongly convicted of a 1989 rape, dubbed the Central Park Five. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5

item.title: Public bathroom access in NYC to be voted on by City Council
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Knowing where the closest bathroom is in New York City can be a challenge, but a new City Council bill may change that. Councilmember Sandy Nurse is leading the push to expand public bathroom access.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11
